# Test Cases

*Source: _qa/risk-strategy.md*

## Module Index

|-----------|----------------|----------|------------|
| MBST | Membership Billing State Transitions | P0 | 8 |
| AUTH | Authentication and Authorization Controls | P0 | 6 |

| SUBS | Subscription Creation and Plan Validation | P1 | 5 |

---

## Membership Billing State Transitions — P0

```yaml
id: TC-MBST-001
title: "Freeze an active membership with freeze_end exactly at the max_freeze_days boundary"
type: functional
level: integration
priority: P0
technique: boundary
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated with a valid access token; a member exists with an active membership on a plan where `max_freeze_days` is 30; today's date is 2026-04-08

**When** the admin sends `PUT /members/{id}/memberships/{mid}` with body `{ "action": "freeze", "freeze_end": "2026-05-08" }` (exactly 30 days from today)

**Then** the API returns HTTP 200
**And** the response body contains `"status": "frozen"`, `"freeze_start": "2026-04-08"`, `"freeze_end": "2026-05-08"`
**And** the membership status in the database is `frozen`

---

```yaml
id: TC-MBST-002
title: "Reject freeze when freeze_end exceeds max_freeze_days by one day"
type: functional
level: integration
priority: P0
technique: boundary
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated with a valid access token; a member exists with an active membership on a plan where `max_freeze_days` is 30; today's date is 2026-04-08

**When** the admin sends `PUT /members/{id}/memberships/{mid}` with body `{ "action": "freeze", "freeze_end": "2026-05-09" }` (31 days from today — one day beyond the limit)

**Then** the API returns HTTP 409 or 422
**And** the response body contains an error message referencing the freeze duration limit
**And** the membership status remains `active` — no state change has occurred

---

```yaml
id: TC-MBST-003
title: "Reject freeze action on a membership that is already frozen"
type: functional
level: integration
priority: P0
technique: state-transition
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated with a valid access token; a member exists with a membership currently in `frozen` status with `freeze_end: 2026-04-30`

**When** the admin sends `PUT /members/{id}/memberships/{mid}` with body `{ "action": "freeze", "freeze_end": "2026-05-15" }`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 409
**And** the response body identifies an invalid state transition
**And** the membership status remains `frozen` with the original `freeze_end` unchanged

---

```yaml
id: TC-MBST-004
title: "Reject unfreeze action on an active membership"
type: functional
level: integration
priority: P0
technique: state-transition
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated with a valid access token; a member exists with an active membership (status `active`)

**When** the admin sends `PUT /members/{id}/memberships/{mid}` with body `{ "action": "unfreeze" }`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 409
**And** the response body identifies an invalid state transition
**And** the membership status remains `active`

---

```yaml
id: TC-MBST-005
title: "Cancel an active membership via the lifecycle action"
type: functional
level: integration
priority: P0
technique: state-transition
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated with a valid access token; a member exists with an active membership

**When** the admin sends `PUT /members/{id}/memberships/{mid}` with body `{ "action": "cancel" }`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 200
**And** the response body contains `"status": "cancelled"`
**And** the membership status in the database is `cancelled`
**And** no further billing events are scheduled for this membership (next_billing_date is null or in the past)

---

```yaml
id: TC-MBST-006
title: "Cancel a frozen membership"
type: functional
level: integration
priority: P0
technique: state-transition
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated with a valid access token; a member exists with a membership in `frozen` status

**When** the admin sends `PUT /members/{id}/memberships/{mid}` with body `{ "action": "cancel" }`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 200
**And** the response body contains `"status": "cancelled"`
**And** the membership is cancelled — freeze fields (`freeze_start`, `freeze_end`) are preserved in the record but billing is stopped

---

```yaml
id: TC-MBST-007
title: "Reject cancel action on an already-cancelled membership"
type: functional
level: integration
priority: P0
technique: state-transition
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated with a valid access token; a member exists with a membership in `cancelled` status

**When** the admin sends `PUT /members/{id}/memberships/{mid}` with body `{ "action": "cancel" }`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 409
**And** the response body identifies an invalid state transition
**And** the membership remains in `cancelled` status

---

```yaml
id: TC-MBST-008
title: "Observe next_billing_date behavior after membership is frozen — exploratory"
type: functional
level: integration
priority: P0
technique: exploratory
automation_candidate: no
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated with a valid access token; a member exists with an active membership where `next_billing_date` is 2026-04-20; the plan has `max_freeze_days: 30`

**When** the admin freezes the membership with `freeze_end: 2026-05-08` (28-day freeze) and the request succeeds with HTTP 200

**Then** record the value of `next_billing_date` in the response
**And** if `next_billing_date` is still 2026-04-20 — this is a billing defect: the member will be charged during the freeze period. Flag for immediate clarification.
**And** if `next_billing_date` has been extended by 28 days to 2026-05-18 — the system correctly adjusts billing. Document the observed behavior for the coverage summary.

> *Coverage gap: The spec does not document whether freeze extends `next_billing_date`. This test probes observed behavior and requires human interpretation of the result. It cannot be automated until the expected behavior is specified.*

---

> **Excluded:** `past_due` recovery path — no documented API action exists to transition out of `past_due` status. One scenario was written (TC-MBST-008) to probe adjacent behavior. The recovery path gap is carried forward to the coverage summary.

---

## Authentication and Authorization Controls — P0

```yaml
id: TC-AUTH-001
title: "Member successfully authenticates and retrieves their own record"
type: functional
level: system
priority: P0
technique: state-transition
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** a member exists in the system with email `member@example.com` and a known password; the member is not currently authenticated

**When** the member sends `POST /auth/login` with body `{ "email": "member@example.com", "password": "<valid_password>" }`, then uses the returned `access_token` to send `GET /members/{own_id}`

**Then** the login returns HTTP 200 with `access_token`, `refresh_token`, and `expires_in: 3600`
**And** the `GET /members/{own_id}` returns HTTP 200 with the member's own record
**And** the response body does not contain any other member's data

---

```yaml
id: TC-AUTH-002
title: "Reject member access to a different member's record via IDOR"
type: security
level: system
priority: P0
technique: idor
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** two members exist: member A (id `uuid-A`) and member B (id `uuid-B`); member A is authenticated with a valid access token

**When** member A sends `GET /members/uuid-B` (a different member's ID)

**Then** the API returns HTTP 403 Forbidden
**And** the response body does not contain any of member B's personal data (name, email, phone, date_of_birth)
**And** member A's own session remains valid

---

```yaml
id: TC-AUTH-003
title: "Reject staff attempting to create a member (admin-only endpoint)"
type: security
level: system
priority: P0
technique: idor
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** a staff user is authenticated with a valid access token; the staff account has role `staff`, not `admin`

**When** the staff user sends `POST /members` with a valid member creation body

**Then** the API returns HTTP 403 Forbidden
**And** no new member record has been created in the system
**And** the response body does not reveal internal system details

---

```yaml
id: TC-AUTH-004
title: "Reject unauthenticated request to a protected endpoint"
type: security
level: system
priority: P0
technique: idor
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** no Authorization header is present (no token provided)

**When** a request is sent to `GET /members/{id}` without any Authorization header

**Then** the API returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized
**And** the response body does not include any member data
**And** no session or token is created as a side effect

---

```yaml
id: TC-AUTH-005
title: "Obtain a new access token using a valid refresh token"
type: functional
level: integration
priority: P0
technique: state-transition
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** a member is authenticated; the member has a valid `refresh_token` from a previous login; the original `access_token` has expired (or is discarded)

**When** the member sends `POST /auth/refresh` with body `{ "refresh_token": "<valid_refresh_token>" }`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 200 with a new `access_token` and `expires_in: 3600`
**And** the new `access_token` can be used to successfully call `GET /members/{own_id}`
**And** the original `refresh_token` may or may not be rotated — observe and document behavior

---

```yaml
id: TC-AUTH-006
title: "Reject replay of a refresh token after logout"
type: security
level: system
priority: P0
technique: state-transition
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** a member is authenticated and holds a valid `refresh_token`; the member sends `POST /auth/logout` with the `refresh_token` and receives HTTP 204

**When** the member immediately re-sends `POST /auth/refresh` with the same `refresh_token`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized
**And** no new `access_token` is issued
**And** the response body does not reveal details about why the token was rejected (to prevent token probing)

> *Coverage gap: Multi-session behavior (whether logout invalidates all sessions or only the supplied token's session) is undocumented. This test only verifies single-token invalidation. All-session invalidation behavior is not asserted.*

---

## Member Lifecycle and Soft Delete Integrity — P1

```yaml
id: TC-MEMB-001
title: "Create a member with valid unique data"
type: functional
level: system
priority: P1
technique: equivalence
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated with a valid access token; no member exists in the system with the email `newmember@example.com`

**When** the admin sends `POST /members` with body `{ "email": "newmember@example.com", "first_name": "Alex", "last_name": "Rivera", "date_of_birth": "1990-06-15" }`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 201
**And** the response body contains a `Member` object with an assigned `id` (uuid), `status: "active"`, and the submitted field values
**And** the `phone` field is null (not provided)
**And** `created_at` is populated with the current timestamp

---

```yaml
id: TC-MEMB-002
title: "Reject member creation when email already exists on an active member"
type: functional
level: system
priority: P1
technique: equivalence
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated; a member with email `existing@example.com` and status `active` already exists

**When** the admin sends `POST /members` with `"email": "existing@example.com"` and otherwise valid fields

**Then** the API returns HTTP 409 Conflict
**And** the response body identifies the email conflict
**And** no new member record has been created

---

```yaml
id: TC-MEMB-003
title: "Soft-delete a member with no active membership"
type: functional
level: integration
priority: P1
technique: state-transition
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated; a member exists with status `active` and no memberships

**When** the admin sends `DELETE /members/{id}`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 204 with no response body
**And** a subsequent `GET /members/{id}` (as admin) returns the member record with `status: "deleted"`
**And** the member record is not physically removed from the database

---

```yaml
id: TC-MEMB-004
title: "Observe membership status after soft-deleting a member with an active membership — exploratory"
type: functional
level: integration
priority: P1
technique: exploratory
automation_candidate: no
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated; a member exists with status `active` and one membership with status `active`

**When** the admin sends `DELETE /members/{id}` and receives HTTP 204

**Then** retrieve the member's membership via `GET /members/{id}/memberships` using admin credentials
**And** if the membership status is still `active` — this is a billing defect: the member is deleted but billing continues. Flag immediately.
**And** if the membership status is `cancelled` — the system correctly halts billing on member deletion. Document observed behavior.

> *Coverage gap: The spec does not document this interaction. This test probes observed behavior and cannot be automated until the expected outcome is specified.*

---

```yaml
id: TC-MEMB-005
title: "Reject member creation with email of a soft-deleted member — exploratory"
type: functional
level: system
priority: P1
technique: equivalence
automation_candidate: no
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated; a member with email `deleted@example.com` exists with status `deleted`

**When** the admin sends `POST /members` with `"email": "deleted@example.com"` and otherwise valid fields

**Then** observe and record the HTTP status code
**And** if 409 — the system treats soft-deleted email addresses as still reserved. Document and note as potential friction for member re-registration.
**And** if 201 — the system allows email reuse after soft-delete. Confirm the new member is independent of the old record with a different `id`.
**And** if any other code — document the response and escalate for clarification.

> **Excluded:** `suspended` member status has no documented creation or exit path in the API. Test cases for suspended status cannot be written without specification. This gap is carried forward to the coverage summary.

---

## Subscription Creation and Plan Validation — P1

```yaml
id: TC-SUBS-001
title: "Subscribe a member to a plan with a valid payment method"
type: functional
level: system
priority: P1
technique: equivalence
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated; a member exists with status `active` and no existing memberships; a valid `plan_id` exists for a monthly plan at $49.99/month; a valid `payment_method_id` is on file for the member

**When** the admin sends `POST /members/{id}/memberships` with body `{ "plan_id": "<plan_id>", "payment_method_id": "<payment_method_id>" }`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 201
**And** the response body contains a `Membership` object with `status: "active"`, `start_date` equal to today's date, and `next_billing_date` set to one month from today
**And** the member now has exactly one membership

---

```yaml
id: TC-SUBS-002
title: "Reject subscription creation when member already has an active membership"
type: functional
level: system
priority: P1
technique: equivalence
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated; a member exists with an existing membership in `active` status

**When** the admin sends `POST /members/{id}/memberships` with a valid plan and payment method

**Then** the API returns HTTP 409 Conflict
**And** the response body identifies the conflict
**And** the member's existing membership is unchanged — status remains `active`, `next_billing_date` is unchanged
**And** no second membership record has been created

---

```yaml
id: TC-SUBS-003
title: "Reject subscription creation when member already has a frozen membership"
type: functional
level: system
priority: P1
technique: equivalence
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated; a member exists with an existing membership in `frozen` status

**When** the admin sends `POST /members/{id}/memberships` with a valid plan and payment method

**Then** the API returns HTTP 409 Conflict
**And** the frozen membership is unchanged
**And** no additional membership record has been created

---

```yaml
id: TC-SUBS-004
title: "Observe behavior when subscribing with a non-existent payment_method_id — exploratory"
type: functional
level: system
priority: P1
technique: exploratory
automation_candidate: no
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated; a member exists with no existing memberships; a valid plan exists; the `payment_method_id` provided is a well-formed UUID that does not exist in the system

**When** the admin sends `POST /members/{id}/memberships` with the non-existent `payment_method_id`

**Then** observe and record the HTTP status code and response body
**And** if 422 — the system validates payment method existence at creation time. Document as correct behavior.
**And** if 201 — the system creates the membership without validating the payment method. A billing failure will occur at the next billing cycle. This is a deferred billing risk — flag for product clarification.

> *Coverage gap: Payment method validation at subscription time is not documented. This test probes observed behavior only.*

---

```yaml
id: TC-SUBS-005
title: "Prevent duplicate membership creation under concurrent requests"
type: performance
level: system
priority: P1
technique: concurrency
automation_candidate: maybe
```

**Given** an admin is authenticated; a member exists with status `active` and no existing memberships; two simultaneous `POST /members/{id}/memberships` requests are prepared with identical valid bodies

**When** both requests are sent simultaneously (within the same millisecond window, e.g., via parallel HTTP clients)

**Then** exactly one request returns HTTP 201 and creates a membership
**And** the other request returns HTTP 409 Conflict
**And** the member has exactly one membership record — no duplicate was created
**And** there is no orphaned partial membership record in the database

> *Automation candidate marked `maybe`: requires a test harness capable of firing parallel HTTP requests and inspecting database state post-execution.*

---

## Check-in Gate Enforcement — P2

```yaml
id: TC-CHKI-001
title: "Successfully check in a member with an active membership"
type: functional
level: system
priority: P2
technique: equivalence
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** a staff user is authenticated; a member exists with a membership in `active` status; a valid `location_id` exists for one of the 12 gym locations

**When** the staff user sends `POST /check-ins` with body `{ "member_id": "<member_id>", "location_id": "<location_id>" }`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 201
**And** the response body contains a `CheckIn` object with `member_id`, `location_id`, and a `checked_in_at` timestamp near the current time
**And** the check-in record is persisted and retrievable via `GET /members/{id}/check-ins`

---

```yaml
id: TC-CHKI-002
title: "Reject check-in for a member with a frozen membership"
type: functional
level: system
priority: P2
technique: equivalence
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** a staff user is authenticated; a member exists with a membership in `frozen` status

**When** the staff user sends `POST /check-ins` with the member's ID and a valid `location_id`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 409 Conflict
**And** the response body communicates the reason (membership is not active)
**And** no check-in record has been created for this member

---

```yaml
id: TC-CHKI-003
title: "Reject check-in for a member with a cancelled membership"
type: functional
level: system
priority: P2
technique: equivalence
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** a staff user is authenticated; a member exists with a membership in `cancelled` status

**When** the staff user sends `POST /check-ins` with the member's ID and a valid `location_id`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 409 Conflict
**And** the response body communicates the reason
**And** no check-in record has been created

---

```yaml
id: TC-CHKI-004
title: "Reject check-in for a member with no membership at all"
type: functional
level: system
priority: P2
technique: equivalence
automation_candidate: yes
```

**Given** a staff user is authenticated; a member exists with status `active` but has never been subscribed to any plan (no membership records)

**When** the staff user sends `POST /check-ins` with the member's ID and a valid `location_id`

**Then** the API returns HTTP 409 (per spec documentation)
**And** no check-in record has been created
**And** observe whether the response body distinguishes between "member not found" (404) and "no active membership" (409) — the spec states 409 but the distinction in message wording matters for staff UX

> **Excluded:** Multi-location concurrent check-in — out of scope; no concurrency specification provided for check-ins. Check-in history date filtering — low risk, excluded per global exclusions.
